LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PENSCO, INC. AND JAMES C. GUCKIAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF REA RENE LAMPARAS-MCCULLOUGH'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE 1ST AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION APPEAR REDUNDANT FOR THE CLAIMS APPEAR ONLY TO BE BASED ON FEHA. IF PLAINTIFF RELIES ON DIFFERENT FEHA SECTIONS, PLAINTIFF SHOULD SO STATE. GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPHS 9,11 AND PAGE 3, LINES 17-19. PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT A CONTINUING VIOLATIONS CLAIM. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

REPLY MEMO OF P&A IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES FILED BY DEFENDANT PENSCO, INC. (DBA PENSCO TRUST COMPANY)
EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT PENSCO, INC. (DBA PENSCO TRUST COMPANY)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PENSCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO PREVAIL ON EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE DISCHARGE AND THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HARASSMENT. THE DIRECTOR'S CONDUCT WAS SUFFICIENTLY PERVASIVE AND SEVERE TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A CAUSAL NEXUS BETWEEN HER PROTECTED CONDUCT AND THE ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION, HER LEAVE. DEFENDANT WAS OBLIGATED TO PLACE PLAINTIFF ON LEAVE IN RESPONSE TO HER DOCTOR'S NOTES AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED A BASIS THAT SHE WAS ENTITLED TO PAID LEAVE. THE COMMON LAW CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DUPLICATIVE BUT ARE NOT BARRED BY FEHA. WORKER'S COMPENSATION DOES NOT BAR THE NEGLIGENT INFLICTION CLAIM BECAUSE OF THE EXTREME NATURE OF THE DIRECTOR'S CONDUCT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 AND 7 ARE OVERRULED. THE FOLLOWING EVIDENCE GOES TO PLAINTIFF'S STATE OF MIND AND EXPLAINS HER DELAY IN REPORTING THE ACTS OF HARASSMENT: 2, 3, 4, 5 AND 7. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE EVIDENCE OFFERED IN THE REPLY IS GRANTED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION A S TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-15-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED DENYING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND DENYING IN PART, AND GRANTING IN PART, DEFT PENSCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF LAMPARAS-MCCULLOUGH, REA RENE

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JAMES GUCKIAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. ASSUMING ARGUENDO THE BURDEN WAS SHIFTED, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE RELEVANT TO HER STATE OF MIND AS WELL AS ADMISSIBLE TO PROVE THE CONDUCT AT ISSUE. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE EVIDENCE OFFERED BY DEFENDANT IN REPLY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PENSCO, INC. AND JAMES C. GUCKIAN'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF REA RENE LAMPARAS-MCCULLOUGH'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED IN PART, OVERRULED IN PART, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED RETALIATORY CONDUCT BY GUCKIAN; 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED. AN EMPLOYEE MAY BE LIABLE FOR RETALIATION BUT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED RETALIATORY CONDUCT BY GUCKIAN. 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION SUSTAINED. NEEDS TO PLEAD FACTS SUPPORTING THE CONTENTION THAT THE ACTS WERE WITHIN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF EMPLOYMENT. 8TH AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION OVERRULED AS TO PENSCO, INC., SUSTAINED AS TO 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO GUCKIAN BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED NEGLIGENT ACTS OR OMISSIONS BY GUCKIAN. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Poppen of Meis & Associates (981.4612) for plaintiff and responding party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JAMES GUCKIAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has failed to shift the burden of producing evidence. The MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied for failure to shift the burden of producing evidence. Assuming arguendo the burden was shifted, plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff presents admissible evidence relevant to her state of mind as well as admissible to prove the conduct at issue. The Court granted plaintiff's motion to strike evidence offered by defendant in reply. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PENSCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Joseph James Poppen, Esq. of Law Offices Meis & Associates 981-4612 for pltf & opposing party; Jamie Rudman, Esq. of Simpson, Garrity & Innes 650/615-4860 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO PREVAIL ON EACH CAUSE OF ACTION.
THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSTRUCTIVE DISCHARGE AND THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HARASSMENT. THE DIRECTOR'S CONDUCT WAS SUFFICIENTLY PERVASIVE AND SEVERE TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A CAUSAL NEXUS BETWEEN HER PROTECTED CONDUCT AND THE ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION, HER LEAVE. DEFENDANT WAS OBLIGATED TO PLACE PLAINTIFF ON LEAVE IN RESPONSE TO HER DOCTOR'S NOTES AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED A BASIS THAT SHE WAS ENTITLED TO PAID LEAVE. THE COMMON LAW CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DUPLICATIVE BUT ARE NOT BARRED BY FEHA. WORKER'S COMPENSATION DOES NOT BAR THE NEGLIGENT INFLICTION CLAIM BECAUSE OF THE EXTREME NATURE OF THE DIRECTOR'S CONDUCT.
DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 AND 7 ARE OVERRULED.
THE FOLLOWING EVIDENCE GOES TO PLAINTIFF'S STATE OF MIND AND EXPLAINS HER DELAY IN REPORTING THE ACTS OF HARASSMENT: 2, 3, 4, 5 AND 7.
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE EVIDENCE OFFERED IN THE REPLY IS GRANTED.
In addition, the Court granted the motion for summary adjudication as to the 4th cause of action. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PENSCO, INC. AND JAMES C. GUCKIAN'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF REA RENE LAMPARAS-MCCULLOUGH'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Laura E. Innes, Esq. of Simpson, Garrity & Innes 650/615-4860 for defts & moving parties; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUSTAINED IN PART, OVERRULED IN PART, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED RETALIATORY CONDUCT BY GUCKIAN; 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION SUSTAINED. AN EMPLYEE MAY BE LIABLE FOR RETALIATION BUT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED RETALIATORY CONDUCT BY GUCKIAN. 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION SUSTAINED. NEEDS TO PLEAD FACTS SUPPORTING THE CONTENTION THAT THE ACTS WERE WITHIN THE COURSE AND SCOPE OF EMPLOYMENT. 8TH AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION OVERRULED AS TO PENSCO, INC., SUSTAINED AS TO 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION AS TO GUCKIAN BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED NEGLIGENT ACTS OR OMISSIONS BY GUCKIAN. Defendant to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
